tests.summary: The Azure session store removes expired sessions during explicit cleanup.
tests.intent: Protect cleanup behavior against real blob listing and deletion semantics.
tests.behavior: Expired sessions are deleted and no longer load after cleanup runs.

tests.summary: The Azure session store round-trips the persisted session state through Azurite.
tests.intent: Protect the live blob serialization path rather than only the in-memory contract.
tests.behavior: Saving then loading a session preserves the key runtime fields and conversation content.

tests.summary: The Azure session store rejects stale writes after a newer version is persisted.
tests.intent: Protect optimistic concurrency for resumable session state.
tests.behavior: A stale copy of the same session key fails with a version mismatch after a newer save succeeds.

tests.summary: The S3 session store removes expired sessions during explicit cleanup.
tests.intent: Protect cleanup behavior against real bucket listing and deletion semantics.
tests.behavior: Expired sessions are deleted and no longer load after cleanup runs.

tests.summary: The S3 session store round-trips the persisted session state through MinIO.
tests.intent: Protect the live S3 serialization path rather than only the in-memory contract.
tests.behavior: Saving then loading a session preserves the key runtime fields and conversation content.

tests.summary: The S3 session store rejects stale writes after a newer version is persisted.
tests.intent: Protect optimistic concurrency for resumable session state.
tests.behavior: A stale copy of the same session key fails with a version mismatch after a newer save succeeds.

tests.summary: AgentBuilder preserves the configured composition surface when building an agent.
tests.intent: Protect the low-ceremony composition API used by consumer code.
tests.behavior: Builder output keeps model, instructions, tools, handoffs, MCP definitions, metadata, model settings, and output contracts.

tests.summary: AgentRunRequest helper methods preserve resume and continuation metadata.
tests.intent: Keep request helper ergonomics aligned with the underlying runtime contracts.
tests.behavior: Helper APIs carry session, previous-response, and approval-resume data into the resulting request.

tests.summary: Completed runs persist the final output back into the session conversation.
tests.intent: Protect the persisted run transcript contract for resumable execution.
tests.behavior: Completed runs return a final output and save the final-output item into the stored conversation.

tests.summary: Configured max turns stop the run before unbounded looping.
tests.intent: Protect callers from runaway tool-call or model loops.
tests.behavior: Runs return MaxTurnsExceeded once the configured turn budget is exhausted.

tests.summary: Handoffs switch execution to the delegated agent.
tests.intent: Protect runtime routing between cooperating agents.
tests.behavior: When a handoff is requested the runner invokes the handoff and reports the delegated agent as the final agent.

tests.summary: Tool calls execute and feed their outputs into the continuing run.
tests.intent: Protect the basic tool-execution loop in the runner.
tests.behavior: Tool calls invoke the configured tool and append tool output items before the next turn completes.

tests.summary: Streaming runs emit run items as they are generated.
tests.intent: Protect the streaming contract used by interactive callers.
tests.behavior: Streaming execution emits the initial user input, generated message items, and the final output item in-order.

tests.summary: Persisted approval state can be resumed from a stored session snapshot.
tests.intent: Protect durable approval workflows that span process boundaries.
tests.behavior: Stored sessions expose pending approval state that can be resumed and completed later.

tests.summary: Approval-required tool calls pause execution until the caller resumes the run.
tests.intent: Protect the approval workflow for sensitive tool invocations.
tests.behavior: Approval-required tool calls return ApprovalRequired first and only execute after an approval response is supplied.

tests.summary: Tool guardrail tripwires stop execution before the tool runs.
tests.intent: Protect enforcement of per-tool input guardrails.
tests.behavior: Tripwired tool input guardrails return GuardrailTriggered and surface the guardrail message on the result.

tests.summary: Run-level input guardrails execute before the first turn.
tests.intent: Protect run-wide preflight validation semantics.
tests.behavior: Run-level input guardrails can stop execution before any turn executor output is accepted.

tests.summary: Rejected approvals use the configured tool error formatter.
tests.intent: Protect the caller-facing rejection message contract.
tests.behavior: Rejected approval items use the supplied tool error formatter instead of invoking the tool.

tests.summary: Ensures the attribute can be applied to classes and methods more than once.
tests.intent: Protect the trait-annotation contract used by requirement-home tests.
tests.behavior: The attribute reports class and method targets and allows multiple instances.

tests.summary: Ensures the attribute stores the requested evidence classification.
tests.intent: Protect the coverage-type payload carried by the trait.
tests.behavior: The attribute exposes the selected coverage classification on the CoverageType property.

tests.summary: Ensures multiple coverage classifications can annotate the same method.
tests.intent: Protect the multi-coverage annotation pattern used by mixed-evidence tests.
tests.behavior: Two coverage-type attributes remain attached in declaration order.

tests.summary: Ensures the trait discoverer emits the expected xUnit trait key and value.
tests.intent: Protect the trait discovery contract used by xUnit filtering.
tests.behavior: The discoverer emits a single `CoverageType` trait keyed by the enum value name.

tests.summary: Verifies custom session stores can replace the default runtime store without being versioned.
tests.intent: Protect the extension hook used by future non-file backends.
tests.behavior: A custom `IAgentSessionStore` is used by the runner and does not require cleanup support.

tests.summary: Verifies the Azure adapter registers the same runtime session store contracts as the file backend.
tests.intent: Protect the Azure package DI surface and ensure it can replace the core store cleanly.
tests.behavior: `AddAzureAgentSessions` resolves a concrete Azure store and the generic session interfaces to the same instance.

tests.summary: Verifies the extensions package wires the runnable default stack for DI consumers.
tests.intent: Protect the public DI registration surface in Incursa.OpenAI.Agents.Extensions.
tests.behavior: The container resolves runnable services and persists sessions through the file-backed store.

tests.summary: Verifies the configured API key is applied to the named OpenAI audio HttpClient.
tests.intent: Protect direct credential configuration for the audio DI surface without requiring environment variables.
tests.behavior: `OpenAiAudioOptions.ApiKey` sets the bearer token on the configured audio client.

tests.summary: Verifies the configured API key is applied to the named OpenAI HttpClient.
tests.intent: Protect direct credential configuration without requiring environment variables.
tests.behavior: `OpenAiResponsesOptions.ApiKey` sets the bearer token on the configured OpenAI client.

tests.summary: Verifies the S3 adapter registers the same runtime session store contracts as the file backend.
tests.intent: Protect the S3 package DI surface and ensure it can replace the core store cleanly.
tests.behavior: `AddS3AgentSessions` resolves a concrete S3 store and the generic session interfaces to the same instance.

tests.summary: Verifies composite extension observers fan out each runtime observation to all registered sinks.
tests.intent: Protect the extensions observability surface from dropping observations when multiple sinks are registered.
tests.behavior: Each sink receives the same observation exactly once.

tests.summary: Tool discovery is cached when MCP tool-list caching is enabled.
tests.intent: Protect the caching contract for streamable MCP clients.
tests.behavior: Repeated tool discovery calls reuse the cached result instead of issuing a second HTTP request.

tests.summary: Streamable MCP requests apply dynamic auth headers and metadata on each request.
tests.intent: Protect per-user MCP request shaping for hosted application scenarios.
tests.behavior: Requests carry resolver-provided bearer tokens, dynamic headers, and metadata payload content.

tests.summary: Transient MCP failures retry and emit observations when retry settings allow it.
tests.intent: Protect resiliency and observation behavior for MCP tool calls.
tests.behavior: Retryable failures schedule a retry, eventually succeed, and emit retry and success observations.

tests.summary: Speech-generation requests map repo-owned settings onto the upstream SDK options and normalize the binary output.
tests.intent: Protect the public speech-generation surface from drifting away from the upstream OpenAI SDK.
tests.behavior: Speech requests preserve model, voice, format, and tuning options while returning audio bytes and format metadata.

tests.summary: Non-seekable streams are still supported after local validation.
tests.intent: Keep server-side streaming scenarios viable while still performing empty-input and header checks.
tests.behavior: Validation peeks the header from non-seekable streams and replays the consumed bytes to the upstream SDK.

tests.summary: Transcription requests map repo-owned settings onto the upstream SDK options and normalize the result using a real microset fixture.
tests.intent: Protect the public audio transcription surface from drifting away from the upstream OpenAI SDK.
tests.behavior: Transcription requests preserve model, prompt, language, timestamp choices, and normalized response data.

tests.summary: Empty audio streams are rejected before any SDK client is created.
tests.intent: Prevent pointless uploads and clearer caller failures for empty inputs.
tests.behavior: Seekable empty streams fail local validation before network work begins.

tests.summary: Known audio headers are checked before upload when content sniffing is enabled.
tests.intent: Catch mismatched or obviously invalid binary data before it reaches the OpenAI API.
tests.behavior: Files whose bytes do not match the declared extension fail local validation before network work begins.

tests.summary: Oversized seekable streams are rejected before upload.
tests.intent: Enforce a configurable local size cap instead of relying on remote failures.
tests.behavior: Seekable streams larger than the configured maximum fail local validation before network work begins.

tests.summary: Unsupported file extensions are rejected before upload.
tests.intent: Block clearly invalid input types at the boundary of the public audio API.
tests.behavior: Files outside the configured extension allowlist fail local validation before network work begins.

tests.summary: Invalid output schemas are rejected before the request reaches the OpenAI SDK.
tests.intent: Prevent malformed output schemas from surfacing as remote 400 responses.
tests.behavior: Constructing an output contract with a non-object schema fails locally.

tests.summary: Wrapping typed options does not force snapshot serialization for SDK-only tool types.
tests.intent: Prevent request construction from crashing on hosted MCP tools due to SDK serializer limitations.
tests.behavior: Creating an internal request from typed options succeeds and leaves the compatibility JSON body empty until explicitly provided.

tests.summary: Handoff normalization can strip pre-handoff tool-call items from mapped model input.
tests.intent: Protect run-level handoff history shaping in the adapter.
tests.behavior: Normalized model input omits pre-handoff function-call and function-call-output items when that mode is enabled.

tests.summary: Reasoning item IDs can be omitted from mapped input when configured.
tests.intent: Protect request-shaping options for reasoning items.
tests.behavior: Mapped reasoning items omit their IDs when the configured reasoning-item policy is Omit.

tests.summary: Run-level model input filters are applied during request mapping.
tests.intent: Protect consumer control over the model-visible conversation input.
tests.behavior: Mapped input reflects the filtered conversation instead of the full stored conversation.

tests.summary: Hosted MCP approval requests are surfaced as approval-required tool calls.
tests.intent: Protect MCP approval handling after switching to the official Responses SDK item models.
tests.behavior: MCP approval request output items map to approval-required MCP tool calls with parsed arguments.

tests.summary: Structured final outputs keep the caller-supplied CLR type metadata for downstream consumers.
tests.intent: Preserve typed output metadata after removing CLR-based schema generation.
tests.behavior: Mapped final output carries the output contract CLR type when one is supplied.

tests.summary: Runtime execution uses typed SDK options instead of rebuilding them from the JSON snapshot.
tests.intent: Prevent the compatibility request snapshot from becoming the live transport source of truth.
tests.behavior: When typed options and a snapshot disagree, the client uses the typed options for the SDK call.

tests.summary: Turn execution resolves local MCP servers into OpenAI tool definitions before sending the request.
tests.intent: Protect local MCP server translation in the OpenAI adapter.
tests.behavior: Resolved streamable MCP tools are added to the request body before the turn completes.

tests.summary: Factory-based session-store replacement removes the versioned registration and uses the supplied store.
tests.intent: Protect the non-versioned session-store override hook.
tests.behavior: `AddAgentSessionStore(Func<IServiceProvider, IAgentSessionStore>)` resolves the supplied store and removes the versioned-store registration.

tests.summary: Default file-backed sessions wire the versioned store and keep the retention defaults intact.
tests.intent: Protect the default file-session overload and its retention baseline.
tests.behavior: `AddFileAgentSessions(string)` registers the file store as both session interfaces and leaves the default retention options unchanged.

tests.summary: Configured OpenAI audio registration applies the base address to the named client.
tests.intent: Protect the HttpClient configuration path used by OpenAI audio.
tests.behavior: `AddOpenAiAudio(Action<OpenAiAudioOptions>)` flows the configured base address to the named OpenAI audio client.

tests.summary: Default OpenAI audio registration exposes the named client without requiring configuration.
tests.intent: Protect the default OpenAI audio DI surface.
tests.behavior: `AddOpenAiAudio()` registers the named client without requiring a configuration callback.

tests.summary: Configured OpenAI Responses registration applies the base address to the named client.
tests.intent: Protect the HttpClient configuration path used by OpenAI Responses.
tests.behavior: `AddOpenAiResponses(Action<OpenAiResponsesOptions>)` flows the configured base address to the named OpenAI client.

tests.summary: Default OpenAI Responses registration exposes the named clients and default option shape without configuration.
tests.intent: Protect the default OpenAI Responses DI surface.
tests.behavior: `AddOpenAiResponses()` registers the named clients without requiring a configuration callback.

tests.summary: Factory-based versioned-store replacement keeps the session and versioned interfaces aligned.
tests.intent: Protect the versioned session-store override hook.
tests.behavior: `AddVersionedAgentSessionStore(Func<IServiceProvider, IVersionedAgentSessionStore>)` resolves the supplied versioned store and wires `IAgentSessionStore` to it.

tests.summary: Authentication failures do not retry and surface as `McpAuthenticationException`.
tests.intent: Protect correct classification of non-retryable MCP auth failures.
tests.behavior: Unauthorized responses throw `McpAuthenticationException` without performing additional attempts.

tests.summary: JSON-RPC server errors surface as helpful MCP server exceptions.
tests.intent: Protect caller-facing diagnostics for MCP server-side failures.
tests.behavior: JSON-RPC error responses throw `McpServerException` with the server message included.

tests.summary: Dynamic tool filters can hide tools and tolerate filter failures without leaking blocked tools into the final tool list.
tests.intent: Protect runtime tool visibility filtering for hosted MCP discovery.
tests.behavior: Allowed tools remain visible while blocked tools and filter failures are excluded from the final tool list.

tests.summary: HttpClient construction rejects calls that do not carry a bearer authorization header.
tests.intent: Protect the SDK handoff guard so consumers get an immediate local failure.
tests.behavior: Constructing the client without a bearer authorization header fails before any request is sent.

tests.summary: Endpoint resolution keeps the documented base URI behavior for blank, responses, and v1-responses paths.
tests.intent: Protect the client endpoint normalization contract used by the OpenAI transport adapter.
tests.behavior: Null base addresses fall back to the OpenAI default endpoint, blank and `/responses` paths keep the base address, `v1/responses` keeps the version prefix, and unsupported paths are rejected.

tests.summary: API failures retain the response body details when the OpenAI SDK raises a client error.
tests.intent: Protect the public exception surface so troubleshooting includes the upstream response body.
tests.behavior: OpenAI client failures are wrapped with the upstream response body appended to the error message.

tests.summary: Request mapping keeps the model, continuation, tools, handoffs, hosted MCP tools, and structured output contract.
tests.intent: Protect the core OpenAI Responses request-mapping contract used by turn execution.
tests.behavior: Mapped requests preserve model selection, previous-response continuation, tool payloads, handoff routing, hosted MCP metadata, and structured-output schema details.

tests.summary: Request mapping preserves request metadata, tool schemas, handoff translation, hosted MCP translation, and model-setting patches.
tests.intent: Protect the public request shape that is sent to the OpenAI Responses API.
tests.behavior: Mapped requests keep metadata, preserve explicit tool schemas, fall back to default tool schemas, honor handoff overrides, translate hosted MCP tools, and ignore explicit top-level transport fields in model settings.

tests.summary: Reasoning items preserve the joined summary text, status, encrypted content, and omission policy.
tests.intent: Protect reasoning-item fidelity when mapped into the OpenAI Responses SDK model.
tests.behavior: Reasoning input preserves the readable summary text, keeps status and encrypted content, and omits the reasoning item ID when requested.

tests.summary: Custom model-setting patches survive SDK transport serialization and reach the wire body.
tests.intent: Prove that the public model-settings dictionary is not just retained in-memory but actually serialized into the outgoing Responses request.
tests.behavior: Custom model-setting keys are serialized into the OpenAI Responses request body while explicit top-level transport fields remain controlled by the mapper.

tests.summary: Streaming execution reconstructs completed function-call arguments for emitted tool-call run items and final tool-call results.
tests.intent: Protect streamed function-call fidelity when argument fragments complete later in the stream.
tests.behavior: Completed function arguments appear on emitted tool-call run items and on the final response tool-call data.

tests.summary: The release versioning script can advance from a tagged commit when the working tree is dirty.
tests.intent: Protect release versioning for local release preparation on top of an already-tagged HEAD commit.
tests.behavior: When HEAD already has a release tag and tracked changes exist, the script continues instead of failing on the existing HEAD tag guard.

tests.summary: The release versioning script can preview the computed version without changing the repository.
tests.intent: Support a safe check for what the next release would be before committing to any release action.
tests.behavior: When calculate-only mode is used, the script reports the computed bump and exits before validation or repo mutation.

tests.summary: The release versioning script can honor a manual minor bump override when the public API diff looks breaking.
tests.intent: Protect the explicit bump override path for semver exceptions that are intentionally non-breaking.
tests.behavior: When the shipped API diff would normally require a major bump, the script respects -Bump Minor and the validator accepts it.

tests.summary: Verifies the composite MCP observer fans out each observation to all registered sinks.
tests.intent: Protect the extensions MCP observability surface from partial sink delivery.
tests.behavior: Each sink receives the same observation exactly once.

tests.summary: Verifies the runtime observer receives the lifecycle events emitted by a successful run.
tests.intent: Protect the runtime observability contract that downstream sinks rely on.
tests.behavior: The observer receives the run and turn lifecycle events in the emitted observation stream.

tests.summary: Generated contracts from CLR types produce explicit strict object schemas for the core runtime.
tests.intent: Protect the optional extensions-based convenience path after removing schema generation from the core package.
tests.behavior: Generated contracts keep CLR type metadata and produce strict root and nested object schemas.

tests.summary: Type-based generation honors explicit schema names on the resulting output contract.
tests.intent: Protect the naming surface used by the OpenAI response format.
tests.behavior: Explicit names override the default CLR type name.

tests.summary: The file session store can clean up expired sessions.
tests.intent: Protect cleanup behavior for time-limited durable session storage.
tests.behavior: Expired file-backed sessions are removed and no longer load after cleanup.

tests.summary: The file session store preserves agent, response, and conversation state across loads.
tests.intent: Protect durable local session persistence.
tests.behavior: Saving and loading a file-backed session preserves core run-state fields and conversation items.

tests.summary: The file session store trims the conversation according to the configured retention window.
tests.intent: Protect bounded session growth for file-backed persistence.
tests.behavior: Configured limits trim older conversation items and record the trimmed item count.

tests.summary: The file session store replaces persisted content atomically.
tests.intent: Protect durability without leaving partially written temp files behind.
tests.behavior: Repeated saves leave only the final session file and no lingering temp files.

tests.summary: The in-memory session store returns the saved conversation on later loads.
tests.intent: Protect the default session persistence contract.
tests.behavior: Saving a session to the in-memory store preserves its conversation for later retrieval.

tests.summary: The in-memory versioned session store rejects stale writes.
tests.intent: Protect optimistic concurrency for resumable sessions.
tests.behavior: Saving an older session version throws a version mismatch exception after a newer write succeeds.
